title: paspalum furcatum var. villosum
intro: Paspalum furcatum var. villosum, also known as the hairy paspalum, is a type of grass that belongs to the Paspalum genus and is native to tropical and subtropical regions. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of various health conditions. The hairy paspalum is reported to have health benefits such as soothing digestive issues and reducing fever, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of decoctions, infusions, or dried extracts, typically combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
